prompt: Wide three-quarter exterior view of the finished composite deck behind a 1990s Haverhill split-level. Warm-gray composite decking with hidden fasteners, clean square edges, two-step elevation down to the lawn. Western red cedar railing posts and top rail with thin black powder-coated balusters. A pair of dark powder-coated steel chairs and a small grill at the back end of the deck. Trimmed lawn behind. Shot with a 24mm wide architectural lens at low three-quarter angle, late afternoon golden-hour sun rakes across the boards casting long honest shadows from the balusters. A red Stihl trimmer leans against the foundation as the only fully saturated color. Slightly desaturated, NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
